intent: R3.0 Step0 shape attribution for post-R3.1 Cijk/Tensile GEMM hotspot before TunableOp/Inductor A/B.

run_notes.driver_exit_reason: The first driver used a 900s health timeout; vLLM became healthy shortly after the timeout, so a salvage request reused the already-ready locked service.

The successful run used the installed competition wheel plus the diagnostic probe path only.

python_shape_probe.verdict: Do not use Python-level UnquantizedLinearMethod.apply hook as the primary Cijk attribution source under torch.compile; the language-model request runs through the compiled graph.

model_static.layer_pattern: 3 linear_attention layers followed by 1 full_attention layer, repeated

compiled_graph_projection_families.priority: low for R3.0 prefill GEMM autotune

call_count_reconciliation.interpretation: The post-R3.1 Cijk call count is fully explained by compiled language-model projection GEMMs plus one logits GEMM per chunk.

hipprof_cijk_rows.attribution: one 48-layer linear_attention projection family across 4 chunks
hipprof_cijk_rows.confidence: medium-high: exact call-count match; exact WGM-to-shape mapping still needs M/N/K from Tensile/TunableOp logs

hipprof_cijk_rows.attribution: mixed projection families sharing the same Tensile kernel name
hipprof_cijk_rows.confidence: medium: call count is a mixture; treat as MLP plus GDN/output-projection tuning target, not a single module

likely logits/lm_head, one per prefill chunk; low priority for this card

main_findings: The hot Cijk group is not a single op. It is the compiled language-model dense projection set: all-layer MLP gate/up and down projections, GDN input/output projections, full-attn qkv/o projections, plus a tiny lm_head tail.
The total Cijk call count (1220) is exactly reconciled by 4 prefill chunks x compiled projection families (1216) plus 4 lm_head/logits calls.
The Python-level linear hook is a negative diagnostic: it only sees visual warmup shapes under torch.compile, so future attribution should use compiled graph / profiler / TunableOp records.
verdict.next_step: Run task-r3.0 Step1 PyTorch TunableOp A/B first on 8-16K throughput-only. Require a tunable result file or equivalent M/N/K record covering the target shapes; if empty or no coverage, stop TunableOp and try Inductor GEMM autotune.
